## Browse by task

### asr

- [AISHELL-1](./aishell.md) — 170-hour open Mandarin speech corpus recorded in clean studio conditions; the standard Chinese ASR benchmark.
- [AMI Meeting Corpus](./ami.md) — ~100 h of recorded English meetings with synchronized audio, video, and rich annotations including transcripts and speaker labels.
- [Common Voice](./commonvoice.md) — Mozilla's crowd-sourced multilingual ASR corpus covering 100+ languages; size, quality, and demographics vary widely by language.
- [FLEURS](./fleurs.md) — Few-shot Learning Evaluation of Universal Representations of Speech — standardised ASR/LID evaluation set covering 102 languages derived from the FLoRes-200 text corpus.
- [GigaSpeech](./gigaspeech.md) — 10,000-hour multi-domain English ASR corpus spanning audiobooks, podcasts, and YouTube.
- [KeSpeech](./kespeech.md) — 1,542 h from 27,237 speakers across 34 cities, covering standard Mandarin and its 8 subdialects with transcription, speaker, and subdialect labels.
- [KsponSpeech](./ksponspeech.md) — ~969 h of Korean spontaneous open-domain dialogue from ~2,000 native speakers, with dual orthographic + pronunciation transcription.
- [Libri-Light](./libri_light.md) — ~60k h of unlabelled English read speech from LibriVox audiobooks, with small labelled subsets (10h, 1h, 10min) for limited-supervision ASR.
- [LibriSpeech](./librispeech.md) — Read English audiobooks; the standard English ASR benchmark.
- [Multilingual LibriSpeech](./mls.md) — 50,000-hour multilingual audiobook ASR corpus derived from LibriVox recordings covering 8 languages (English, German, Dutch, French, Spanish, Italian, Portuguese, Polish).
- [People's Speech](./peoples_speech.md) — 30,000-hour English ASR corpus assembled from diverse internet sources including radio broadcasts, court hearings, and conferences.
- [SPGISpeech](./spgispeech.md) — 5,000 h of professionally transcribed English company earnings-call audio, fully formatted with punctuation and capitalization.
- [Switchboard-1 Release 2](./switchboard.md) — ~2,400 two-sided spontaneous English telephone conversations among 543 US speakers (~260 h), separated into two channels.
- [TED-LIUM 3](./tedlium3.md) — 452-hour English ASR corpus of TED talks with manual and automatic transcriptions; suitable for lecture/talk domain ASR research.
- [VoxPopuli](./voxpopuli.md) — Multilingual corpus from 2009-2020 European Parliament recordings: a large unlabelled set across 23 languages plus transcribed speech and aligned interpretations.
- [WenetSpeech](./wenetspeech.md) — 10,000-hour large-scale Mandarin ASR corpus collected from YouTube and podcasts with automatic labelling.

### augmentation

- [MUSAN](./musan.md) — 109-hour corpus of music, speech, and environmental noise designed for data augmentation in speech and speaker recognition experiments.

### multilingual

- [Common Voice](./commonvoice.md) — Mozilla's crowd-sourced multilingual ASR corpus covering 100+ languages; size, quality, and demographics vary widely by language.
- [Emilia](./emilia.md) — Large-scale multilingual in-the-wild speech dataset designed for expressive and diverse TTS training, covering 6 languages.
- [FLEURS](./fleurs.md) — Few-shot Learning Evaluation of Universal Representations of Speech — standardised ASR/LID evaluation set covering 102 languages derived from the FLoRes-200 text corpus.
- [KeSpeech](./kespeech.md) — 1,542 h from 27,237 speakers across 34 cities, covering standard Mandarin and its 8 subdialects with transcription, speaker, and subdialect labels.
- [Multilingual LibriSpeech](./mls.md) — 50,000-hour multilingual audiobook ASR corpus derived from LibriVox recordings covering 8 languages (English, German, Dutch, French, Spanish, Italian, Portuguese, Polish).
- [VoxLingua107](./voxlingua107.md) — ~6,628 h across 107 languages of short segments automatically extracted from YouTube and labelled by video metadata, for spoken language ID.
- [VoxPopuli](./voxpopuli.md) — Multilingual corpus from 2009-2020 European Parliament recordings: a large unlabelled set across 23 languages plus transcribed speech and aligned interpretations.

### speaker

- [AMI Meeting Corpus](./ami.md) — ~100 h of recorded English meetings with synchronized audio, video, and rich annotations including transcripts and speaker labels.
- [CN-Celeb](./cnceleb.md) — 1,200-hour multi-genre Mandarin speaker recognition corpus spanning 11 real-world scenarios collected from Chinese celebrities.
- [Switchboard-1 Release 2](./switchboard.md) — ~2,400 two-sided spontaneous English telephone conversations among 543 US speakers (~260 h), separated into two channels.
- [VCTK](./vctk.md) — 44-hour English multi-speaker corpus with 110 speakers covering a wide range of UK and US accents; widely used for multi-speaker TTS and speaker adaptation research.
- [VoxCeleb2](./voxceleb2.md) — 2,442-hour large-scale speaker recognition corpus with 6,112 celebrities collected from YouTube across many languages.

### tts

- [AISHELL-3](./aishell3.md) — 85-hour multi-speaker Mandarin TTS corpus with 218 speakers in clean recording conditions; the standard Chinese multi-speaker TTS baseline.
- [CSS10](./css10.md) — Single-speaker speech datasets for 10 languages built from aligned public-domain LibriVox clips, intended for TTS.
- [Emilia](./emilia.md) — Large-scale multilingual in-the-wild speech dataset designed for expressive and diverse TTS training, covering 6 languages.
- [Hi-Fi Multi-Speaker English TTS (Hi-Fi TTS)](./hifitts.md) — ~291.6 h high-quality English multi-speaker TTS from 10 LibriVox speakers (>=17 h each), 44.1 kHz, with Project Gutenberg text.
- [LibriTTS](./libritts.md) — High-fidelity (24 kHz) read English audiobooks derived from LibriSpeech, with normalised transcriptions; the standard baseline for English TTS.
- [LJSpeech](./ljspeech.md) — Single-speaker English TTS corpus (24 h, 13,100 clips) recorded from LibriVox readings. Universally used as a single-speaker TTS baseline.
- [VCTK](./vctk.md) — 44-hour English multi-speaker corpus with 110 speakers covering a wide range of UK and US accents; widely used for multi-speaker TTS and speaker adaptation research.
